full_response:
We start from the Einstein equation with cosmological constant Λ (all indices are lowered with the metric g_{ab})  

(1) R_{ab} − ½ R g_{ab} + Λ g_{ab} = 8π T_{ab} .

Here  
R ≡ g^{ab} R_{ab}  is the Ricci scalar,  
T ≡ g^{ab} T_{ab}  is the trace of the energy-momentum tensor,  
and we work in four spacetime dimensions (so g^{ab}g_{ab}=δ^{a}_{a}=4).

Step 1: Take the trace of eq. (1).  
Contract both sides with g^{ab}:

g^{ab}R_{ab} − ½ R g^{ab}g_{ab} + Λ g^{ab}g_{ab} = 8π g^{ab}T_{ab}
 ⟹  R − ½ R (4) + Λ(4) = 8π T
 ⟹  R − 2R + 4Λ = 8π T
 ⟹  −R + 4Λ = 8π T
 ⟹  (2) R = 4Λ − 8π T .

Step 2: Substitute the expression (2) for R back into the original equation (1):

R_{ab} − ½(4Λ − 8π T) g_{ab} + Λ g_{ab} = 8π T_{ab} .

Compute the middle term:

−½(4Λ − 8π T) g_{ab} = −2Λ g_{ab} + 4π T g_{ab} .

Hence

R_{ab} − 2Λ g_{ab} + 4π T g_{ab} + Λ g_{ab} = 8π T_{ab}
 ⟹  R_{ab} − Λ g_{ab} + 4π T g_{ab} = 8π T_{ab}
 ⟹  (3) R_{ab} = 8π T_{ab} + g_{ab}(Λ − 4π T) .

Equation (3) is precisely the desired form.

\boxed{R_{ab} = 8\pi\,T_{ab} + g_{ab}\bigl(\Lambda - 4\pi\,T\bigr)}  

Confidence: 0.91
